accuracy.feedback: The anatomical illustration of the calf muscles and Achilles tendon is generally correct, but the inclusion of the horse image is confusing and unrelated. The labeled muscles appear accurate, but the term 'Three Levels of Treatment' does not correspond to anatomical labels.
issues: Title 'Three Levels of Treatment' does not match anatomical content

style.feedback: The visual style is inconsistent due to the juxtaposition of the anatomical diagram and the unrelated horse illustration. The style seems informal, which may not suit a medical course.
consistency: Inconsistent style between anatomical diagram and horse illustration
Casual style may not align with educational context

patientFriendliness.feedback: While the anatomical diagram is labeled, the presence of the horse image and the unclear title can confuse patients. The purpose of the horse image is not explained, leading to potential misunderstanding.
improvements: Remove or explain the horse illustration
Align title with the anatomical content

recommendations: Remove the horse image to maintain focus on human anatomy.
Ensure the title accurately reflects the content shown in the image.
Use a consistent and professional style suitable for medical education.
